data.title: UMC reports 7% sales growth in June 2025
data.description:
• United Microelectronics Corporation (NYSE:UMC) announced net sales for June 2025, reporting a notable increase compared to the previous year.
  • For the month of June 2025, UMC's revenues reached 18,823,070, marking a 7.26% increase Y/Y from 17,548,413 in June 2024.
  • This positive momentum also extended to the first half of the year, with cumulative revenues for January through June 2025 totaling 116,616,614. This represents a 4.65% rise compared to 111,431,389 recorded in the same period of 2024.
  • UMC's stock price has seen significant gains YTD, rising nearly 21%.
  • However, both Seeking Alpha's quant rating and Wall Street analysts currently assign the stock a Hold rating.

data.title: European markets lose steam ahead of July 9 deadline
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.24% to 8,801.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.53% to 23,808, as nervousness surrounding global trade unsettled investors. Factory orders in Germany dropped by 1.4% M/M in May.

France (CAC:IND) -0.75% to 7,697. Industrial production in France decreased by 0.5% M/M in May.

The annual inflation rate in the Czech Republic rose to 2.9% in June.

Industrial production in Hungary dropped by 2.6% Y/Y in May. 

The Swiss unemployment rate held steady at a non-seasonally adjusted 2.7% in June.

Industrial production in Spain rose 1.7% Y/Y in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) dropped 0.54% to 540.87, marking a loss of 1.88% during the last one month. Market attention shifted back to global trade tensions ahead of the July 9 deadline. U.S. President Trump said that Washington will send formal notifications to trading partners on Friday, detailing the tariff rates they will face on exports to the U.S.

The EU-U.S. trade negotiations wrapped up without a breakthrough. EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic said that while talks would continue. The EU is pushing for an 'agreement in principle' ahead of July 9 but has acknowledged that a comprehensive deal is unlikely to be reached by the deadline

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 6 basis points to 4.35%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 2.59%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 2 basis points to 4.55%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: McEwen Mining announces name change to McEwen Inc.
data.description:
• McEwen Mining (NYSE:MUX) said that it will officially change its name to McEwen Inc., effective July 7, 2025.
  • The company will begin trading on the NYSE under its new name, McEwen Inc., on July 7, 2025, and on the TSX shortly thereafter. There will be no change in the company’s CUSIP or stock symbol.

  • This rebranding reflects the company’s evolving strategy and broader vision for the future.

data.title: Emeren Group announces changes to North America management, gives Q2 update
Vertical Aerospace expands leadership team
data.description:
• Vertical Aerospace (NYSE:EVTL) named new key executives to its leadership team as the company accelerates through its Flightpath 2030 goals.
  • Mark Higson joins as COO, Steve Vellacott joins as VP, Airworthiness & Head of Design Organisation, and Eric Samson named VP Programme-Hybrid.
  • The team expansion is on track to complete its test flight program in 2025, having recently achieved the first piloted wingborne flight of a winged eVTOL in European public airspace.

data.title: U.S. targets Iran oil trade networks with latest sanctions
data.description:
The U.S. has imposed new sanctions against networks that have transported and purchased billions of dollars' worth of Iranian oil, the Treasury Department announced.

The sanctions target a network of companies run by Iraqi businessman Salim Ahmed Said, which profited from smuggling Iranian oil disguised as, or blended with, Iraqi oil since at least 2020. 

Said controls UAE-based VS Tankers, despite avoiding formal association with the company, the Treasury Department noted. Said also owns UAE-based VS Petroleum and Rhine Shipping, as well as UK-based The Willett Hotel and Robinbest.

Several vessels engaged in the covert delivery of Iranian oil have also been sanctioned, intensifying pressure on Iran's "shadow fleet" that enables the regime to transport its petroleum to generate revenue. 

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the department "will continue to target Tehran's revenue sources and intensify economic pressure to disrupt the regime's access to the financial resources that fuel its destabilizing activities."

The Treasury Department also sanctioned several individuals linked to financial institution Al-Qard Al-Hassan, which is considered to be Hezbollah's de facto banking arm.

data.title: Quantum-Si stock falls after pricing capital raise of $50M via stock offering
Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico reports preliminary terminal passenger traffic for June
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacífico, S.A.B. de C.V., (NYSE:PAC) said Thursday the total number of terminal passengers at GAP’s 12 Mexican airports increased by 0.7% in June, compared to a year ago.
  • Los Cabos airport presented an increase in passenger traffic of 1.7%, while Tijuana, Guadalajara, and Puerto Vallarta airports decreased 2.3%, 1.1%, and 0.1% respectively, compared to June 2024.
  • On the other hand, Montego Bay presented a decrease in passenger traffic of 1.9%, compared to June 2024.
  • The seats available during June 2025 increased by 2.1%, compared to June 2024. The load factors for the month went from 83.4% in June 2024 to 82.2% in June 2025.

data.title: Google may be next to settle Trump social media lawsuits after Meta and Musk - report
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) could become the next tech giant after Meta (META) and Elon Musk to settle controversial lawsuits filed by President Donald Trump over the restriction of his social media accounts following the January 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol, according to The Atlantic.

The president alleged that the companies and executives had illegally censored him at the urging of U.S. political leaders, violating his First Amendment rights.

Mark Zuckerberg, the billionaire boss of Meta Platforms (META), settled his case with a payment of $25 million, mostly to Trump’s presidential library fund, while Elon Musk, CEO of Tesla (TSLA) and owner of X (formerly Twitter), followed with $10 million more.

Now it may be Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) CEO Sundar Pichai’s turn.

Lawyers for President Trump and Pichai have begun “productive discussions” about the next steps of the case against YouTube, “with additional discussions anticipated in the near future,” according to briefs filed in a San Francisco federal court shortly after Memorial Day that appear to have escaped public notice, the report said.

The parties have asked the judge to give them until September 2 to come to an agreement on a path forward.

The development follows CBS News parent Paramount Global's (PARA) agreement to pay $16 million to resolve a civil suit over what Trump alleged was the network's deceptive editing of an interview of Vice President Kamala Harris.

data.title: Cboe Global Markets releases June trading volume report
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) on Thursday reported its June 2025 trading statistics, highlighting significant growth across multiple business segments. 

The company set several significant records in its S&P 500 Index options products, including a new quarterly average daily volume (ADV) of 3.7 million contracts and a record 2.2 million contracts in monthly SPX zero-days-to-expiry options.

Key volume metrics show strong year-over-year growth in multiple segments: index options increased 18.6%, U.S. equities on-exchange volume rose 37.7%, and European equities trading grew 22%.
